meta.title: Iconology 2: An Evangelical Assessment of the Byzantine Metamorphosis From Aniconism to Icondulism
meta.author: Joshua Schooping

questions.question: In this talk, which position does Schooping explicitly say he is *not* arguing for?
options: Iconodulia as defined by the Seventh Ecumenical Council
Iconoclasm understood as forbidding or destroying religious imagery in all forms (e.g., illustrated Bibles)
A historical survey of Byzantine art
questions.answer: Iconoclasm understood as forbidding or destroying religious imagery in all forms (e.g., illustrated Bibles)

questions.question: How does Schooping characterize formal Eastern Orthodox iconodulia per the Seventh Ecumenical Council?
options: Optional devotional warmth encouraged but not required
Identical to merely owning religious-themed artwork
Refusal to worship/venerate icons (including bowing and kissing) places one under anathema
questions.answer: Refusal to worship/venerate icons (including bowing and kissing) places one under anathema

questions.question: Which scholarly book does Schooping rely on heavily for the historical survey of images?
options: *History of Icons* by Moshe Barasch (NYU Press)
*On the Divine Images* by John of Damascus
questions.answer: *History of Icons* by Moshe Barasch (NYU Press)

questions.question: According to Schooping’s reading of Porphyry (via Barasch), who “identify the gods with the images”?
questions.answer: Only the uneducated

questions.question: Schooping argues that comparing cult statues to written texts for the illiterate is:
options: A uniquely Christian invention of the fourth century
First introduced by the Seventh Ecumenical Council
A common pagan argument later picked up, while early Christians had rejected that kind of reasoning
questions.answer: A common pagan argument later picked up, while early Christians had rejected that kind of reasoning

questions.question: What shift does Schooping attribute to Iamblichus regarding matter and cult images?
options: Far from treating matter as evil, Iamblichus treats matter as creation; materiality of divine images does not detract from their perceived validity/power
Matter is purely evil, so all images are demonic
Iamblichus banned all public religious art
Iamblichus denied any link between images and gods
questions.answer: Far from treating matter as evil, Iamblichus treats matter as creation; materiality of divine images does not detract from their perceived validity/power

questions.question: According to Schooping (citing Barasch), the iconoclastic debate’s pro-icon arguments would be “unthinkable” without whose philosophical heritage?
questions.answer: Proclus

questions.question: Which figure does Barasch list among early Christian apologists unified in rejecting divine images?
questions.answer: Athenagoras of Athens

questions.question: What time window does Schooping emphasize for Barasch’s claim of unified Christian rejection of sacred images?
How does Barasch (as summarized) treat the idea that Tertullian tacitly permits non-idol religious images because he only explicitly condemns idols?
options: Barasch argues the distinction is hard to maintain; silence about other images should not imply legitimacy
Barasch finds that distinction easy to maintain
Barasch claims Tertullian endorsed church murals
questions.answer: Barasch argues the distinction is hard to maintain; silence about other images should not imply legitimacy

questions.question: What does the third-century Syriac *Didascalia* say about painters and oblations, according to Schooping?
options: Icon painters must lead public worship
Painters receive double honor in the assembly
No oblations may be received from those who paint with colors, make idols, or work gold, silver, and bronze
questions.answer: No oblations may be received from those who paint with colors, make idols, or work gold, silver, and bronze

questions.question: In the pseudo-Clementine church order cited, with whom is a painter grouped?
options: A harlot, brothel-keeper, drunkard, actor, and athlete
questions.answer: A harlot, brothel-keeper, drunkard, actor, and athlete

questions.question: What did Eusebius do in response to Constantia’s request for an image of Christ?
options: He bluntly refused, arguing Christ’s glory cannot be represented in dead colors and such practices are unlawful for Christians
He sent an encaustic icon blessed by local clergy
He commissioned a mosaic for her palace chapel
He referred her to Bishop Athanasius
questions.answer: He bluntly refused, arguing Christ’s glory cannot be represented in dead colors and such practices are unlawful for Christians

questions.question: Schooping invokes Vincent of Lérins as a descriptive rule for orthodoxy meaning:
options: What has been believed everywhere, always, and by all
Only what appears in the Nicene Creed
questions.answer: What has been believed everywhere, always, and by all

questions.question: Schooping claims Julian the Apostate’s developed theory of religious images is later mirrored by:
questions.answer: John of Damascus

questions.question: According to Schooping, Origen (via Basil and Gregory Nazianzen’s compilation) rejects honoring God by fashioning him in:
options: Written scrolls of the Law only
Forms of dead matter, even as supposed pictorial or symbolic representation
questions.answer: Forms of dead matter, even as supposed pictorial or symbolic representation

questions.question: Schooping places the Christian appropriation of pagan defenses of cult statues roughly:
options: Sometime between the third and seventh centuries (with a critical gap bridged mainly by hypothesis)
questions.answer: Sometime between the third and seventh centuries (with a critical gap bridged mainly by hypothesis)

questions.question: The imperial *Divine Sacra* to Pope Hadrian seeks confirmation of:
options: A ban on the Nicene Creed
questions.answer: The “ancient tradition of venerable images”

questions.question: How does Schooping read “anathema” in the Seventh Ecumenical material he quotes (e.g., outer darkness for devil and angels)?
options: Complete separation from God / damnation in the council’s own framing
A purely political oath to the emperor
questions.answer: Complete separation from God / damnation in the council’s own framing

questions.question: Which council teaching line does Schooping quote: honor paid to the image passes to the prototype?
options: The Seventh Ecumenical Council (Nicaea II) material he reads
questions.answer: The Seventh Ecumenical Council (Nicaea II) material he reads

questions.question: Which anathema does Schooping cite against those who call sacred images idols?
options: Anathema to those who call the sacred images idols
There is no such anathema in his reading
Anathema only to those who destroy churches
Anathema to those who deny the Trinity
questions.answer: Anathema to those who call the sacred images idols

questions.question: Schooping argues the council’s treatment of *proskynesis* includes:
options: Only mental assent without bodily acts
Salutation and kissing with the lips, with strong desire toward the holy object
questions.answer: Salutation and kissing with the lips, with strong desire toward the holy object

questions.question: From Psalm 115 (as cited), what makes idols problematic besides being made by human hands?
options: They are always made of iron rather than wood
They have mouths that do not speak, eyes that do not see, ears that do not hear—religious trust in them distorts the soul’s ordering toward God
They are required by Moses for tabernacle worship
questions.answer: They have mouths that do not speak, eyes that do not see, ears that do not hear—religious trust in them distorts the soul’s ordering toward God

questions.question: Schooping says the bronze serpent in the Old Testament is a type of:
questions.answer: Christ, not icons

questions.question: How does Schooping summarize the gospel at the end over against mandatory icon veneration?
options: Salvation through Christ’s blood alone, received by faith alone through grace alone; faithful works through love—not through paint on a board
Salvation through imperial laws on images
Salvation through philosophical mastery of Proclus
questions.answer: Salvation through Christ’s blood alone, received by faith alone through grace alone; faithful works through love—not through paint on a board
